perturbation.original_text: 3. The receiving Party shall not, without prior written permission of the disclosing Party, furnish to any third Party any information, equipment or material embodying or made by use of any Confidential Information <PAGE> -7- received or developed hereunder nor use such information for purposes other than internal evaluation so long as such Confidential Information must be maintained confidential. All Confidential Information in tangible form, and any copies thereof, disclosed hereunder, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party at the disclosing Party's written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Parties request provided that confirmation of destruction is given to the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The receiving Party shall, without prior written permission of the disclosing Party, furnish to any third Party any information, equipment or material embodying or made by use of any Confidential Information <PAGE> -7- received or developed hereunder nor use such information for purposes other than internal evaluation so long as such Confidential Information must be maintained confidential. All Confidential Information in tangible form, and any copies thereof, disclosed hereunder, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party at the receiving Party's written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Parties request provided that confirmation of destruction is given to the disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'not' from the first sentence in section 3 now allows the receiving party to furnish confidential information to a third party. This directly contradicts the original intention of the non-disclosure agreement and creates uncertainty as to which party is responsible for returning documents since it was switched from the disclosing party to the receiving party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The receiving Party shall, without prior written permission of the disclosing Party, furnish to any third Party any information, equipment or material embodying or made by use of any Confidential Information <PAGE> -7- received or developed hereunder nor use such information for purposes other than internal evaluation so long as such Confidential Information must be maintained confidential. All Confidential Information in tangible form, and any copies thereof, disclosed hereunder, shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party at the receiving Party's written request, or destroyed at the disclosing Parties request provided that confirmation of destruction is given to the disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The 'Changed Text' removes 'not' from the sentence 'The receiving Party shall not, without prior written permission of the disclosing Party, furnish to any third Party any information...' which directly contradicts the implied confidentiality of the agreement. The contradicted text is, in effect, the same as the changed text, showing that the removal of the 'not' creates an in-text contradiction. Also, the last sentence in section 3 of both the Changed Text and Contradicted text have the following change in responsibility of returning documents: '...shall be promptly returned to the disclosing Party at the receiving Party's written request...', creating a contradiction to the original section. This fits the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' definition, as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 5. In the event of any breach or threatened breach by the receiving Party of its obligations hereunder, the disclosing Party shall have whatever rights and remedies are available to it at law or in equity, provided, however, that in no event shall either Party be liable for any consequential damages of any nature whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. In the event of any breach or threatened breach by the receiving Party of its obligations hereunder, the disclosing Party shall have whatever rights and remedies are available to it at law or in equity, provided, however, that in no event shall the receiving Party be liable for any consequential damages of any nature whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'either Party' and stating only the 'receiving Party' is not liable for consequential damages creates the implication that the disclosing Party can be held liable for consequential damages, contradicting the original intent where neither party was liable. This introduces a significant imbalance and in-text contradiction regarding liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. In the event of any breach or threatened breach by the receiving Party of its obligations hereunder, the disclosing Party shall have whatever rights and remedies are available to it at law or in equity, provided, however, that in no event shall the receiving Party be liable for any consequential damages of any nature whatsoever.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'either Party' is not liable for consequential damages. The changed text modifies this to state only that the 'receiving Party' is not liable. This creates a direct contradiction because it implies that the disclosing Party *can* be held liable for consequential damages, which conflicts with the original intention and wording where neither party was exposed to such liability. This contradiction is reinforced by the fact that the Contradicted Text is identical to the Original text, and it is clear that the changed text now directly opposes that part of the document.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Neither Party shall disclose Confidential Information nor assign work hereunder to anyone other than the receiving Party's employees who have agreed in writing to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential and to execute all documents deemed reasonably necessary by the disclosing Party to permit compliance with all provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Either Party shall disclose Confidential Information nor assign work hereunder to anyone other than the receiving Party's employees who have agreed in writing to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential and to execute all documents deemed reasonably necessary by the disclosing Party to permit compliance with all provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original section 7 expressly prohibits both parties from disclosing confidential information or assigning work to anyone other than the receiving party's employees. By removing 'Neither' and changing it to 'Either', the revised clause now permits either party to disclose confidential information and assign work, directly contradicting the original non-disclosure intent of the agreement. This creates a significant in-text contradiction because it is not clear whether parties are allowed to disclose confidential information or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Either Party shall disclose Confidential Information nor assign work hereunder to anyone other than the receiving Party's employees who have agreed in writing to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential and to execute all documents deemed reasonably necessary by the disclosing Party to permit compliance with all provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'Neither Party shall disclose Confidential Information,' which clearly prohibits both parties from disclosing confidential information. The changed text replaces 'Neither' with 'Either,' effectively allowing either party to disclose confidential information. This directly contradicts the intended non-disclosure agreement and creates a significant conflict with the contradicted text, which is the same text but after the change. This is a clear and direct conflict, leading to uncertainty about whether parties are allowed to disclose confidential information or not.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Nothing contained in this Agreement shall constitute a commitment by either Party to the development or release of any future products and/or programs disclosed thereby or restrict either Party in its efforts to improve its existing products and systems and to conceive and develop new products and systems. Additionally, participation in the information exchange pursuant to this Agreement shall not constitute or imply a commitment by either Party to favour or recommend any product or service of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Everything contained in this Agreement shall constitute a commitment by either Party to the development or release of any future products and/or programs disclosed thereby or restrict either Party in its efforts to improve its existing products and systems and to conceive and develop new products and systems. Additionally, participation in the information exchange pursuant to this Agreement shall constitute or imply a commitment by either Party to favour or recommend any product or service of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Nothing' to 'Everything', 'not constitute' to 'constitute', and 'not imply' to 'imply', this clause now directly contradicts its original intent. Initially, the agreement stated that the exchange of information does *not* create any commitment to develop or release products, or to favor the other party's services. The revised clause states the opposite: that the agreement *does* constitute a commitment to these actions. This creates significant in-text contradiction and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Everything contained in this Agreement shall constitute a commitment by either Party to the development or release of any future products and/or programs disclosed thereby or restrict either Party in its efforts to improve its existing products and systems and to conceive and develop new products and systems. Additionally, participation in the information exchange pursuant to this Agreement shall constitute or imply a commitment by either Party to favour or recommend any product or service of the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the agreement does *not* create any commitment to develop or release products, or to favor the other party's services. The changed clause states the opposite: that the agreement *does* constitute a commitment to these actions. The contradicted text is a copy of the changed text, which clearly shows that they contradict what the original text stated, therefore a contradiction exists.
